---
title: "Spatial Epidemiology and Applied Data Science"
subtitle: "DMgtAsia · Second Module (Intermediate level)"
author:
  - name: "Prof. (Dr.) Biju Soman"
    affiliation: "AMCHSS, SCTIMST, Trivandrum"
  - name: "Dr. Arun Mitra"
    affiliation: "AIIMS Bibinagar"
date: last-modified
---

![](dmgt_asia_logo.png){width=160 fig-align="left"}

## Welcome

This is the Second Module (Intermediate level) of the DMgtAsia course. It covers spatial
epidemiology and spatial data science in R, from GIS concepts through to disease-cluster
detection on Indian public health datasets.

The material draws on spatial data science trainings and workshops run across India, adapted
for the DMgtAsia course.

## What you will be able to do

- Explain GIS concepts (CRS and projections, spatial dependence, spatial autocorrelation,
  Tobler's First Law) and how they apply to public health.
- Run spatial exploratory data analysis and apply CDC cartographic guidelines to public health
  maps.
- Read, transform and join spatial data with `sf`; extract raster values; geocode addresses;
  and build choropleths.
- Run and interpret global and local spatial autocorrelation (Moran's I, LISA), and map
  SIR/SMR to find disease clusters.

## How the module is organised

The module has four sessions. Each pairs a lecture with hands-on R exercises and a quiz.

| Session | Theme | Lectures | Hands-on |
|------|-------|----------|----------|
| **Session-I** | Foundations of spatial thinking | Intro to GIS · Concepts in SDS | Reflection |
| **Session-II** | Exploratory Spatial Data Analysis (ESDA) | Exploring data with R · Visualization principles | EDA on WHO TB data |
| **Session-III** | Introduction to `sf` | The `sf` package | Exercises 1–4 |
| **Session-IV** | Spatial Data Analysis & Clustering | (lab-led) | Exercises 6–7 · John Snow · SIR/SMR · Capstone |

For the full session list and run order, see the navigation bar, or go to
**[Setup](setup.qmd)** to prepare your machine.

## Delivery

- **Self-paced:** 4 weeks, one session per week.
- **Intensive workshop:** 2 days. Day 1 covers Sessions I–II, Day 2 covers Sessions III–IV.

## Acknowledgements

Developed at the Achutha Menon Centre for Health Science Studies, SCTIMST, and AIIMS Bibinagar.
Built on the `sf`, `spdep`, `rgeoda`, `ggspatial` and `tidyverse` packages. Data: NMC, NFHS,
WHO, SEDAC GPWv4, and `HistData`.
